payload.answer: The new statement may update the existing project from planned/open to implemented, but it could also describe a separate MVP milestone rather than the whole Brain Slack agent. Safe actions available: approve_supersession if the MVP implementation means the existing planned/open project should be replaced or marked no longer current; keep_both if the planned/open agent and implemented MVP are distinct or both should remain; reject_new if the new statement is incorrect or should not be saved; edit if the wording should be clarified before saving.
citations: Existing project state - The Brain Slack agent is planned, status=open
New - The Brain Slack agent MVP is now implemented

entity_resolution.reason: The existing item is the Brain Slack agent, while the new item is the Brain Slack agent MVP. They may refer to the same project or to a narrower MVP milestone, so the relationship should be confirmed before changing durable memory.

memory_cards.statement: The Brain Slack agent is planned, status=open

details: Preserved ambiguity between the full Slack agent and its MVP.
Did not present the existing planned/open state as superseded without approval.
